2026-04-17T08:24:00.4851371Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T08:24:00.4852000Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T08:24:00.4852361Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T08:24:00.4852663Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:24:00.4852894Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T08:24:00.4853047Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-17T08:27:41.5948856Z   
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5949271Z   1. The README doesn't document key resource types like:
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5949896Z      - CognitiveServicesProject
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5950405Z      - CognitiveServicesCapabilityHost
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5951005Z      - CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5951616Z      - Account Connections and Project Connections
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5952069Z      
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5952668Z   2. The README's "Examples" section only links to a generic page rather than having code snippets inline
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5953423Z   
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5954028Z   3. The README's "Key concepts" section is missing - it just points to generic SDK docs
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5954742Z      
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5957332Z   The CHANGELOG also doesn't list the new resource types despite the api-version upgrade, and while sample files exist in the test folder, the README only references a generic samples page instead of showing inline examples for managing Projects, CapabilityHosts, and Connections. I should compare this against the canonical KeyVault README to see what's expected.
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5959881Z 
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5961105Z ✓ $ head -100 /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azure-sdk-for-net/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md

2026-04-17T08:27:41.5963326Z 
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5963775Z ◆ Now I have a good picture of the documentation gaps. Let me summarize:
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5964425Z   
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5966698Z   1. **README - "Key concepts" section is missing service-specific content**: The current README points to generic Azure SDK concepts, but doesn't document the key resources in this package: `CognitiveServicesAccount`, `CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHost`, Connections, etc.
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5968982Z   
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5971275Z   2. **README - "Examples" section lacks inline code snippets**: The README only links to a generic samples page (`https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`). The test Samples/ folder has many sample files (including new ones for Project, CapabilityHost, Connections) but those are not referenced in the README with proper `Snippet:` placeholders.
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5973745Z      
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5977410Z   3. **CHANGELOG needs updating for the 1.6.0-beta.1 release**: The entry currently just mentions the API version upgrade to 2026-03-01, but it's missing documentation of all the new resource types that were actually added—like the new capability host collection and resource patterns, the enhanced `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` with capability host support, and the custom models for connections and capability hosts.
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5980783Z   
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5981868Z   4. **README doesn't document the new resources added in the 2026-03-01 API version**: Projects and their associated capabilities need to be highlighted as key concepts.
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5983126Z   
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5986730Z   The main documentation gaps I need to address are: the README's "Key concepts" section lacks service-specific details about CognitiveServices resources, the "Examples" section only points to a generic page instead of providing inline code snippets for common tasks like account management and working with projects and capability hosts, and the CHANGELOG doesn't fully capture what was added in this TypeSpec migration. I should file an issue to track these gaps.
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5989328Z 
2026-04-17T08:27:41.5991684Z ◆ Now I have enough information to identify the documentation gaps. The README for `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` is minimal — it has no service-specific key concepts, no inline code examples (only a generic link), and the CHANGELOG doesn't list the new resource types added (Projects, CapabilityHosts, Connections). Let me file the issue.

2026-04-17T08:27:41.8350617Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T08:27:23.657892116Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`\n**Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57069) by @live1206\n\n## What Changed\n\nThe package was migrated from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec, upgrading the API version to 2026-03-01. This added several new custom resource types and supporting models:\n\n- `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` — manage AI Foundry Projects under a Cognitive Services account\n- `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` / `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` — manage capability hosts scoped to a project\n- `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` — data model shared by account- and project-scoped capability hosts\n- `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` / `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` — new model types\n- `CognitiveServicesExtensions` — new extension methods on `ArmClient` and `SubscriptionResource`\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **README \"Key concepts\" section is empty of service content** — it links to the generic Azure SDK docs page but does not describe any of the resource types in this package (accounts, projects, capability hosts, connections, deployments, etc.)\n- **README \"Examples\" section has no inline code snippets** — it only links to `https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`. The `tests/Samples/` folder contains 35+ generated sample files (including new ones for `CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, `CognitiveServicesConnection`) but none are wired into the README via `Snippet:` placeholders\n- **CHANGELOG `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is incomplete** — only records \"Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01\" without listing the new resource types (`CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, capability host data models, new extension methods) added by the migration\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### 1a. Update `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md`\n\nEdit the `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to enumerate new resource types:\n\n```markdown\n## 1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01.\n- Added `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` to manage AI Foundry Projects nested under a Cognitive Services account.\n- Added `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` and `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` to manage capability hosts scoped to a project.\n- Added `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` as the shared data model for both account-level and project-level capability hosts.\n- Added `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` and `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` model types.\n- Added `GetCognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` extension method on `ArmClient`.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n### Other Changes\n```\n\n#### 1b. Edit `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md`\n\nReplace the generic **Key concepts** section with service-specific content, and add `Snippet:` placeholders to the **Examples** section. See Step 2 below for the snippet details.\n\nReplace the \"Key concepts\" section body:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n### CognitiveServicesAccountResource\n\nRepresents a top-level Azure Cognitive Services account. Accounts hold AI models, deployments, connections, projects, and capability hosts.\n\n### CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource\n\nRepresents a model deployment within an account. Use this to create, update, or delete deployments of AI models.\n\n### CognitiveServicesProjectResource\n\nRepresents an AI Foundry Project nested within a Cognitive Services account. Projects can have their own connections and capability hosts.\n\n### CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource / CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource\n\nRepresents a capability host attached either to an account or to a project. Capability hosts define the AI capabilities (e.g., storage, vector search) available to the parent resource.\n\n### Thread safety\n\nWe guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.\n\n### Additional concepts\n\u003c!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR --\u003e\n[Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |\n[Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) |\n[Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) |\n[Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |\n[Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) |\n[Mocking](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/unit-testing-mocking) |\n[Client lifetime](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/lifetime-management-and-thread-safety-guarantees-of-azure-sdk-net-clients/)\n\u003c!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR --\u003e\n```\n\nReplace the \"Examples\" section body:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\nThe following examples show common operations using the management library.\n\n### List all Cognitive Services accounts in a subscription\n\n```C# Snippet:CognitiveServices_GetAccounts\n```\n\n### Create or update an account\n\n```C# Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount\n```\n\n### Create a project within an account\n\n```C# Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateProject\n```\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nThe repository uses a snippet-generation tool: write sample code in a test file with `#region Snippet:\u003cName\u003e` / `#endregion`, add a placeholder in the README, then run `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject the code.\n\n#### Snippet 1 — List accounts:
